title: Interactive REPL with reedline
purpose: The interactive REPL is the primary interface for developers using Norn from a terminal. Without it, the CLI can only operate in print mode. This brief delivers the reedline line editor integration, multi-line input, persistent command history, tab completion for slash commands and file paths, a prompt showing model and session state, signal handling for Ctrl+C and Ctrl+D, and multi-turn conversation continuity via EventStore and LoopContext lifecycle management between turns.
task: Replace the placeholder REPL handler (repl.rs at root, from NC-006) with a real reedline-powered interactive loop in a repl/ folder module. Wire reedline with a history file at ~/.local/share/norn/history.txt (XDG-compliant), a custom completer providing tab completion for slash commands (from SlashCommandRegistry) and file paths, a prompt renderer showing the active model name and session status, and signal handling. Each turn reads user input, preprocesses it through preprocess_input(), runs run_agent_step() with the accumulated EventStore, and loops. Between turns, call LoopContext::clear_dynamic_sections() and LoopContext::evaluate_prompt_commands() to refresh dynamic system sections. Ctrl+C sends a cancellation signal to the running agent step via the CancellationToken. Ctrl+D exits the REPL cleanly. NOTE: The crate was reorganised — see DESIGN.md Structure section. Import paths use crate::cli, crate::config, crate::runtime, crate::print, not the old flat module names.

requirements.title: Reedline editor integration
requirements.spec: WHEN the CLI enters interactive REPL mode, the system SHALL construct a reedline::Reedline editor instance with a FileBackedHistory pointing to the XDG data directory (respecting XDG_DATA_HOME, falling back to ~/.local/share/norn/history.txt on Linux and ~/Library/Application Support/norn/history.txt on macOS when XDG vars are unset). The editor SHALL be configured with the NornCompleter (R3) and NornPrompt (R4).
acceptance: Running `norn` in a TTY presents a reedline prompt that accepts input
Up-arrow recalls previous commands from history
History file is created at the XDG-compliant path on first use
History persists across REPL sessions (exit and re-enter, up-arrow shows prior input)

requirements.title: Multi-line input
requirements.spec: WHEN the user presses Alt+Enter (default newline keybinding) while composing input in the REPL, the system SHALL insert a newline into the current buffer instead of submitting. WHEN the user presses Enter, the system SHALL submit the complete multi-line buffer as a single input.
acceptance: Pressing Alt+Enter inserts a newline; the prompt continues on the next line
Pressing Enter after multi-line input submits the entire buffer
Multi-line input is passed intact to preprocess_input() and then to the agent

requirements.title: Tab completion for slash commands and file paths
requirements.spec: WHEN the user presses Tab while typing input that begins with '/', the system SHALL offer completions from the set of registered slash command names in the SlashCommandRegistry (both CLI built-ins and profile-registered commands). WHEN the user presses Tab while typing input that does not begin with '/', the system SHALL offer file path completions relative to the current working directory. The completer SHALL implement reedline::Completer.
acceptance: Typing '/he' then Tab completes to '/help'
Typing '/co' then Tab offers '/compact' and '/config' (if registered)
Typing 'src/' then Tab lists files in the src/ directory
Tab completion for slash commands includes all registered commands (built-ins + profile)

requirements.title: Prompt rendering with model name and session status
requirements.spec: The system SHALL implement reedline::Prompt to display a prompt containing the active model name and a session status indicator (number of turns or token usage). The prompt format SHALL be '{model_name} [{turn_count}] > ' where turn_count is the number of completed user turns in the current session. WHEN no model is set, the system SHALL display 'norn' as the model name.
acceptance: REPL prompt shows the model name (e.g. 'gpt-5.5 [0] > ')
After each turn, the turn count increments in the prompt
When no --model is specified and no profile sets a model, prompt shows 'norn [0] > '

requirements.title: Ctrl+C interrupts current agent step
requirements.spec: WHILE an agent step is executing (run_agent_step is in progress), IF the user presses Ctrl+C, the system SHALL cancel the current step via the tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken passed to run_agent_step. The REPL SHALL remain active after cancellation, displaying a 'Cancelled.' message and returning to the prompt for the next input.
acceptance: Pressing Ctrl+C during a long-running agent step cancels it within 1 second
After cancellation, the REPL prompt reappears and accepts new input
'Cancelled.' is printed to stderr after the step is cancelled
The EventStore retains events from the cancelled step (partial work is not discarded)

requirements.title: Ctrl+D exits the REPL
requirements.spec: WHEN the user presses Ctrl+D at an empty prompt (EOF signal), the system SHALL exit the REPL loop cleanly, flush any pending session persistence, and exit with code 0.
acceptance: Pressing Ctrl+D at an empty prompt exits the REPL
Exit code is 0 after Ctrl+D
Session is persisted before exit (if session persistence is enabled)

requirements.title: Multi-turn EventStore continuity
requirements.spec: The REPL loop SHALL maintain a single EventStore instance across all turns within the session. WHEN the user submits input, the system SHALL append a UserMessage event to the store, call run_agent_step with the accumulated store, and loop back for the next input. The messages vector SHALL be rebuilt from the store's events before each run_agent_step call.
acceptance: A second turn's agent call includes context from the first turn
EventStore.len() increases monotonically across turns
The agent can reference information from earlier turns in its responses

requirements.title: LoopContext lifecycle between turns
requirements.spec: WHEN the REPL completes an agent step and returns to the prompt for the next turn, the system SHALL call LoopContext::clear_dynamic_sections() followed by LoopContext::evaluate_prompt_commands() before the next run_agent_step call. This ensures rule injections and prompt-command outputs are refreshed each turn.
acceptance: Dynamic system sections from rules do not accumulate across turns
Prompt commands re-execute between turns (or serve cached values within TTL)
The system instruction string changes between turns when prompt commands produce different output

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement streaming display rendering -- that is NC-007
SHALL NOT implement slash command handlers -- that is NC-006; the REPL calls preprocess_input() but does not define command handlers
SHALL NOT implement session persistence (JSONL read/write) -- that is NC-002
SHALL NOT implement provider construction or agent configuration -- that is NC-003 and NC-004
SHALL NOT use ratatui alternate screen -- output is inline per DESIGN.md NG1
SHALL NOT implement print mode execution -- that is NC-003
verification: cargo clippy -p norn-cli -- -D warnings produces zero warnings
cargo fmt -p norn-cli -- --check produces no diff
cargo test -p norn-cli passes all tests
Running `norn` in a TTY shows a reedline prompt with model name
Up-arrow recalls history from prior sessions
Tab on a file path prefix lists directory contents
Ctrl+C during agent execution cancels and returns to prompt
Ctrl+D at empty prompt exits with code 0
No file exceeds 500 lines of code
No code in mod.rs beyond pub mod declarations and re-exports
